From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

WW2 Royal Italian Army formation

The **XI Army Corps** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *XI Corpo d'Armata*) was a corps of the [Royal Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") that participated in the [invasion of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Invasion_of_Yugoslavia "Invasion of Yugoslavia").[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTENafziger19971–7-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=XI_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The XI Army Corps was created in [Bari](/wiki/Bari "Bari") on 8 July 1883, and fought in World War I, after which it was dissolved.

In April 1939, [Italy occupied Albania](/wiki/Italian_invasion_of_Albania "Italian invasion of Albania") and on 9 November 1940, the occupation troops, known until then as *Comando Superiore Truppe Albania*, were renamed XI Army Corps, and became part of the [2nd Army](/wiki/2nd_Army_(Italy) "2nd Army (Italy)"). The Corps participated in the [Greco-Italian War](/wiki/Greco-Italian_War "Greco-Italian War") without much success.

On 11 April 1941, the Corps advanced into Yugoslav territory, reaching and occupying Ljubljana. After the Yugoslav capitulation on 23 April, the Corps remained in Slovenia as an occupation force with its headquarters in Ljubljana. It was involved in anti-partisan operations and brutal repression of the population.

During 1942, the XI Army Corps was moved to Greece as part of the Italian occupation army there. On 25 July 1943, the Corps became part of the German [OB Südost](/wiki/OB_S%C3%BCdost "OB Südost") and was based in [Thessaloniki](/wiki/Thessaloniki "Thessaloniki").

XI Army Corps was disarmed by the Germans and dissolved on 18 September 1943 after the [Armistice of Cassibile](/wiki/Armistice_of_Cassibile "Armistice of Cassibile").

## Commanders

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=XI_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Commanders")]

* [Matteo Roux](/wiki/Matteo_Roux "Matteo Roux") (1940.01.01 – 1940.11.01)
* [Mario Robotti](/wiki/Mario_Robotti "Mario Robotti") (1940.11.01 – 1942.12.15)
* [Gastone Gambara](/wiki/Gastone_Gambara "Gastone Gambara") (1942.12.15 – 1943.09.08)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=XI_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-FOOTNOTENafziger19971–7_1-0)** [Nafziger 1997](#CITEREFNafziger1997), pp. 1–7.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** ["Regio Esercito"](https://web.archive.org/web/20231130004256/http://www.regioesercito.it/reparti/fanteria/recda11.htm). Archived from [the original](http://www.regioesercito.it/reparti/fanteria/recda11.htm) on 2023-11-30. Retrieved 2022-07-14.

Combined Arms Research Library Digital Library. Archived from [the original](http://www.cgsc.edu/CARL/nafziger/941IDAC.PDF) (PDF) on 22 March 2014. Retrieved 20 July 2013.
